purpose: Local dev tool that reports whether newer versions of the three pinned native dependencies (mermaid, cmark-gfm, WebView2 SDK) are available upstream. Parses MERMAID_VERSION / CMARK_GFM_VERSION / WEBVIEW2_VERSION from tools/install-native-deps.py, queries the matching registries, and prints a pinned-vs-latest table. Informational only — bumping a pinned version still requires a manual edit to install-native-deps.py so v1.x releases stay reproducible and any breaking upstream change is caught by the user's normal test pass before tagging.
exports: read_pinned() — returns {MERMAID_VERSION, CMARK_GFM_VERSION, WEBVIEW2_VERSION} parsed from install-native-deps.py
fetch_json(url, timeout) — minimal urllib wrapper that sets a User-Agent header
CHECKS tuple — (label, env-var name, fetcher) triples driving the report
main() — prints the comparison table; always returns exit code 0
invariants: Always exits 0 — never fails a build or hook when a registry is briefly unreachable; failures surface as 'fetch failed: <err>' in the table.
Pinned-version regex is anchored to ^NAME = "..." lines so only the literal constants at the top of install-native-deps.py are parsed; comments and other matches are ignored.
WebView2 selection filters out pre-release tags (any version containing '-') so 'latest' only reports stable upgrades.
gotchas: If install-native-deps.py renames or reformats the version constants (e.g. uses single quotes, or moves them into a dict), the regex returns '?' and the script silently reports the pinned value as missing. Keep the constants as `NAME = "value"` at module scope.
GitHub's unauthenticated API gives 60 req/hour per IP — fine for human use, would need an Authorization header if folded into CI.
NuGet flat-container returns versions oldest-first by convention. The script trusts that ordering; if NuGet ever changes the contract this picks the wrong 'latest'.
